title: Export of API demo mapping tool to json / csv?
author: machabees78
body: Github neophyte here, so apologies if I missed something obvious.  Is there a way to export BSSIDs found using the demo API / mapping tool?  Aim would be a seamless means to feed located BSSIDs from the API demo tool into Apple's WPS API.  Thanks.

title: Android phone
author: mx-info
body: Have you ever tried an Android phone, like a Samsung, or similar devices? Do they also have a similar location tracking mechanism?

title: Searching for nearby interfaces via BSSID seems to have stopped working.
author: NENCAO
body: I’ve noticed that the method of scanning nearby devices via BSSID works only within China; in other regions, nothing can be discovered anymore. What could be causing this?

title: I created a web app using your research
author: christianrowlands
body:
First, thank you for doing this research and figuring out the Apple API. It is super cool that you did this work and shared it open source.

Next, I wanted to share that I took the work that you did for the Apple API and made a web app. The source code is here:

https://github.com/christianrowlands/wavedigger

And the website is hosted here (if you don't want to run it locally):

https://wavedigger.networksurvey.app/

I am not requesting anything in this issue; I just wanted to share the code that I wrote using your code and research.

title: go concurrent bssid scanner
author: randomizedcoder
body:
Thanks for this repo!

I "borrowed" code from this repo to implement a go concurrent BSSID scanner.

https://github.com/randomizedcoder/go-bssid-geolocator

Using this overnight I scanned and found ~91million
```
[das@t:~/Downloads]$ cat 2025_03_18_1640 | grep -v generateMACs | grep -v "Lat: -180.000000 Long: -180.000000" | wc -l
91273223
[das@t:~/Downloads]$ ls -lah 2025_03_18_1640
-rw-r--r-- 1 das users 9.2G Mar 19 14:50 2025_03_18_1640
```
Example of output
```
<snip>
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 9c:5:d6:b:0:2 found at Lat: 41.567009 Long: 2.015902
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 6a:1e:19:9f:be:b6 found at Lat: 41.566376 Long: 2.015917  <--- Location = Barcelona 
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 34:57:60:b4:42:7e found at Lat: 41.566753 Long: 2.014822
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 38:22:e2:54:a7:c7 found at Lat: 41.566376 Long: 2.015649
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 3e:93:b2:7f:2e:d0 found at Lat: 41.567039 Long: 2.015907
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 40:ae:30:86:74:4e found at Lat: 41.566380 Long: 2.015485
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 40:ae:30:86:74:aa found at Lat: 41.566505 Long: 2.015411
2025/03/18 17:15:17 Worker id:15 BSSID: 44:ff:ba:2d:88:d0 found at Lat: 41.567024 Long: 2.015716
<snip>
```

If you are interested, possibly I could send you an MR to add my code into your repo

Thanks again

title: Need a little clarification on spoofing
author: terryjools
body:
I configured mitmproxy with the wloc.py script and after that the problems started. There seems to be a mistake in the description, you point to /cmd/fakeloc/main.go, I configured the server in /cmd/spoofed/main.go.
The problem is that it receives a request and gives a response, which is swapped in mitmproxy, but the location doesn't change, it just says it couldn't get the location, although everything is fine according to the log.

Here is spoofed log:

2025/03/10 11:36:16 000100000001000000190a1134323a37353a63333a66393a61313a3339f80101800202

2025/03/10 11:36:20 8a:d5:9d:58:e9:9d
2025/03/10 11:36:20 
2025/03/10 11:36:28 Recieved request
2025/03/10 11:36:28 7a:d5:9d:57:c3:cd
2025/03/10 11:36:28 aa:d5:9d:57:c3:cd
2025/03/10 11:36:28 0001000000010000008712350a1137613a64353a39643a35373a63333a636412200880d4dae60e10d8c7c3f6d8ffffffff011827200328920430e807583f60d30312350a1161613a64353a39643a35373a63333a636412200880d4dae60e10d8c7c3f6d8ffffffff011827200328920430e807583f60d3030a1133633a37633a33663a65343a37323a3463f80102800202

title: How i run map tile

body: Help me to run this code and see perticular wifi in given geo code. I am new to this language or any python code for run or any database file

title: Notes on where to find things
author: acheong08
body:
Extract dyld shared cache.
`https://www.jviotti.com/2023/11/20/exploring-macos-private-frameworks.html`

`_CLPNRCellTowerLocationReadFrom`. `_CLPWifiAPLocationReadFrom` : CoreLocationProtobuf from Private frameworks.

title: URLS
author: acheong08

title: Other things to look into
author: acheong08
body:
**https://gsp85-ssl.ls.apple.com/wifi_request**
 - Comes from `com.apple.geod`
 - Sends MAC address and gets a `fixed64` back. (Does 1804284399684943872 mean anything to anyone?)
